title: Tinea pedis review

red_flags.text: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports documenting severe pain, diabetes, immunosuppression, spread, secondary infection and systemic illness only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

investigations.text: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording laboratory results already available without generating microscopy, culture or biopsy.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

follow_up.text: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.
follow_up.rationale: The exact UAE DHA section supports documenting a tinea-pedis review, interval course, itch, scale, fissures, maceration, redness, vesicles, ulcers, foot distribution, clinician inspection and impact without inferring active fungal infection.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

source_limitations: Exact sites and morphology, nail or body spread, diabetes and immune status, prior treatment and response, diagnosis, KOH or culture, medicine, dose, duration, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
